concerns.text: FR-013 (VIF collinearity diagnostics) and FR-010 (associational findings) lack explicit implementation tasks. T045 implements VIF but conditions it on a `--model-method=regression` flag not defined in the spec or plan. There is no task to implement the logic enforcing 'associational' language in outputs (FR-010) or to handle the specific regression mode trigger defined in FR-013.

FR-014 (Exclusion Rate) is mentioned in T028, but there is no task explicitly calculating the exclusion rate (percentage of tasks excluded due to missing test suites or generation failures) and writing it to the final summary as required. T028 is vague on the output format and location compared to the strict requirement in FR-014.

SC-003 (Sensitivity Analysis Sign Flip) requires the system to verify that the sign of the mean coverage gap does not flip across thresholds. T029 implements the sensitivity analysis but lacks a specific task to programmatically verify the 'no sign flip' condition and fail the build or flag the result if it occurs, leaving the success criterion unverified.

FR-009 requires that for HumanEval, the system must 'measure and report only line coverage' and log branch coverage as N/A. T012 states the system will 'execute pytest --cov' (which measures both) and then 'validate and log branch_coverage as N/A'. This implies measuring branch coverage unnecessarily, potentially violating the constraint to avoid invalid comparisons. A task to explicitly skip branch coverage measurement for HumanEval is missing.

T045 references a `--model-method` flag and 'regression' mode which are not defined in the spec (FR-013) or plan. The task description relies on a non-existent system capability, creating a traceability gap. The task should be updated to reflect the actual trigger condition defined in FR-013 (multi-variable regression model performance).
